title: pedicularis roylei subsp. megalantha
intro: Pedicularis roylei subsp. megalantha is a subspecies of flowering plant in the balsam family, Pedicularis roylei, that is native to the Himalayas. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
